intro: The 8-layer hierarchy above is the general shape of every drink — but the exact technique changes a lot by drink family. Pick a category to see how specific, real menu items actually get built, step by step, based on Starbucks' own published recipes and barista training material.

drinks.name: Caffè Latte
steps: Pull 1–2 shots of espresso directly into the cup
Steam milk to about 150°F, texturing just a thin layer of microfoam
Pour the steamed milk over the espresso
Finish with a thin spoonful of foam on top

drinks.name: Cappuccino
drinks.ratio: the "50/50 rule" — half foam, half milk
steps: Pull 1–2 shots of espresso directly into the cup
Steam milk into a thick, dense microfoam
Pour milk and foam together over the espresso
Foam should hold roughly half the cup's volume

drinks.name: Caramel Macchiato
steps: Pour vanilla syrup into the bottom of the cup
Add steamed milk, textured with foam on top
Pull espresso shots and pour them on top, "marking" through the foam
Finish with a crosshatch of caramel drizzle

drinks.name: Pike Place® Roast (drip)
drinks.ratio: no milk, no espresso — just brewed coffee
steps: Grind beans fresh for drip brewing
Hot water passes through the grounds in the drip brewer
Brewed coffee holds in an airpot, decanted to order
Poured straight into the cup

drinks.name: Cold Brew
steps: Coarsely grind beans specifically for cold steeping
Steep in cold (never iced or hot) filtered water for up to 20 hours
Filter the grounds out of the concentrate
Serve over ice — naturally sweeter and less acidic than hot-brewed coffee

drinks.name: Nitro Cold Brew
drinks.ratio: poured with no ice at all
steps: Start from the same 20-hour slow-steeped Cold Brew
Store in a chilled keg, pressurized with nitrogen
Pour from a tap, the same way a stout beer is poured
Nitrogen microbubbles create a cascading, velvety pour with a creamy head

drinks.name: Coffee Frappuccino (e.g. Mocha, Caramel)
steps: Combine ice, milk, and Frappuccino coffee base in the blender
Add the flavored syrup or sauce
Blend until smooth and slushy — a xanthan-gum base syrup keeps it from separating
Top with whipped cream and a drizzle

drinks.name: Crème Frappuccino (e.g. Vanilla Bean, Strawberries & Crème)
steps: Swap the coffee base for a sweetened, caffeine-free crème base
Blend with ice, milk, and flavoring
Same thickened, slushy texture as a coffee Frappuccino — without any coffee
Top with whipped cream

drinks.name: Strawberry Açaí / Mango Dragonfruit Refresher
steps: Add a scoop of freeze-dried fruit "inclusions" to the shaker
Pour in the fruit-juice Refreshers base, which carries green coffee extract for caffeine
Add water (or lemonade) plus ice
Shake to combine — the same shaken method used for iced tea

categories.note: Swap the water for coconutmilk and any Refresher becomes a Pink Drink-style variant — same build, different base liquid.

drinks.name: Chai Tea Latte
steps: Fill the cup about halfway with Tazo chai concentrate and hot water
Steam milk (2% to about 170°F)
Pour steamed milk in, holding back the foam with a spoon
Scoop foam on top and finish with a dusting of cinnamon

drinks.name: Matcha Latte
steps: Add classic syrup to the bottom of the cup
Whisk matcha powder into milk with a back-and-forth motion until fully dissolved
Steam the matcha-milk mixture together
Pour the steamed matcha milk over the syrup

drinks.name: Iced Brown Sugar Oatmilk Shaken Espresso
drinks.ratio: shaken for a micro-foam, not stirred
steps: Stir brown sugar into hot espresso so it fully dissolves
Add the sweetened espresso and ice to a shaker with a dash of cinnamon
Shake vigorously 10–20 times — this chills the drink and whips a light foam
Pour over ice and top with oatmilk

drinks.name: Pumpkin Spice Latte
drinks.ratio: a Latte build with a seasonal sauce
steps: Pour pumpkin spice sauce into the cup
Pull espresso shots on top and stir together
Fill the rest of the cup with steamed milk
Top with whipped cream and pumpkin spice topping

categories.note: Most seasonal drinks reuse a core method already on this page — usually the Latte or Frappuccino build — with a seasonal syrup or sauce swapped in.

These aren't built by a barista at all. RTD bottles and cans are manufactured and sealed off-site through Starbucks' PepsiCo (North America) and Nestlé (global CPG) partnerships, then shipped to store shelves and grocery channels ready to drink — no in-store sequence applies.

drinks.name: Pour-Over
steps: Grind beans fresh, coarser than an espresso grind
Rinse the paper filter and add the grounds
Pour hot water in slow, deliberate circles, blooming and saturating the grounds evenly
Full manual control produces a clean cup with no sediment or oil

drinks.name: Nitro Reserve
steps: Use the same nitrogen-infusion process as Nitro Cold Brew, with single-origin Reserve beans
Store in a chilled, pressurized keg
Pour slowly from the tap over about 20 seconds
Produces the same cascading, velvety pour — just with Reserve-grade coffee
